home.title: OAuth Client Helper
home.description: Assists you throughout the process of creating an OpenID Connect (OAuth) client and sharing it.
home.chooseGrantType: I just want to choose a grant type

notFound.title: Oops! Page Not Found!
notFound.description: It seems like the page you're looking for does not exist or might have been removed.

grantTypes.pageTitle: Choose the right OAuth Grant Type
grantTypes.metaDescription: Find out which OAuth 2.0 grant type is right for your application.

clients.metaDescription: View your recently created OAuth clients.
clients.noClients: No clients. Create one to get started.

createClient.title: Create your new client
createClient.metaDescription: Create a new OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect client step by step.
createClient.subtitle: Choose the type of application you are building and the grant type you want to use.
createClient.name: Name
createClient.nameDescription: Usually your application friendly name. Will be displayed in the authorization page.
createClient.redirectUrisDescription: List of redirect URIs (comma separated) that your client can redirect to.
createClient.allowedCorsOriginsDescription: List of allowed CORS origins (comma separated) for your JavaScript Application.
createClient.scopesDescription: List of scopes (comma separated) that your client can request.
createClient.audiencesDescription: List of audiences (comma separated) that your client can request.
createClient.postLogoutRedirectUrisDescription: List of post logout redirect URIs (comma separated) that your client can redirect to after logout.
createClient.contactsEmailDescription: List of contacts email (comma separated) for your client.
createClient.logoUriDescription: The Logo URI of your application. Will be displayed in the authorization page.
createClient.tokenEndpointAuthMethodDescription: The Token Endpoint Authentication Method of your application.
createClient.grantTypesDescription: The grant types your client can use.
createClient.dataUsageDescription: The data you provide here won't be send to our servers. Only browser local storage and URL encoding is used to store and share the data.

clientView.developerDescription: Create the real client can take some time. If you want to test the client right now, you can create a fake test client.
clientView.testClientDialogDescription: This feature is not available yet. We are working on it.
clientView.emailBody: Please we would need you to create a new OAuth2 client. You can check the following link for all the client details:

templates.librariesIntro: To implement OpenID Connect for {name}, you can use one of the following libraries:
templates.grantTypesIntro: {name} can use one or many of the following OAuth Grant Types:
templates.authMethodsTitle: Which Token Endpoint Authentication Methods for {name}?
templates.authMethodsIntro: {name} can use one or many of the following OAuth Token Endpoint Authentication Methods:

useCases.title: OpenID Connect and IAM Use Cases
useCases.description: Read about common use cases for OpenID Connect and IAM

iamProviders.title: (C)IAM Identity Providers benchmark
iamProviders.description: Compare Identity and Access Management providers across authentication methods, user management, compliance, security, and developer integration features.
iamProviders.lookingForOpenID: Looking for OpenID Connect protocol-level compatibility?
iamProviders.checkOpenID: Check out the OpenID Connect Providers benchmark
iamProviders.compareNote: If you want to compare IAM features of different providers, please check out the
iamProviders.lookingForOpenIDProvider: Looking for {name}’s OpenID Connect protocol compatibility?
iamProviders.viewOnOpenID: View {name} on the OpenID Connect Providers benchmark
iamProviders.lookingForOpenIDGeneral: Looking for OpenID Connect protocol-level compatibility across providers?
iamProviders.faqDeprecated: {name} supports {featureName}, but it is deprecated or not recommended anymore.

survey.title: Find the best (C)IAM solution for your business
survey.description: Answer a few questions about your use case and we'll recommend the best identity providers for you.
survey.personalizedRecommendationsText: Answer a few questions to get personalised vendor recommendations.
survey.scaleAffectsPricing: Scale significantly affects platform choice and pricing models.

step_audience.title: Who are your users?
step_audience.subtitle: This helps us match vendors to your identity use case.

b2c.label: Consumers / Customers (B2C)
b2c.description: End-users signing up for your product — social login, passkeys, magic links.

b2b.label: Businesses / Enterprise customers (B2B)
b2b.description: Teams and organisations — enterprise SSO, SAML, multi-tenancy.

b2e.label: Employees / Workforce (B2E)
b2e.description: Internal users — Active Directory, SAML, adaptive MFA.

mixed.label: Multiple audiences
mixed.description: A mix of the above — we'll balance across all dimensions.

step_scale.title: What's your expected scale?
step_scale.subtitle: Monthly active users (MAU) affect pricing and platform choice significantly.

step_deployment.title: How do you want to run it?
step_deployment.subtitle: Deployment model affects control, compliance, and total cost.

saas.label: Cloud SaaS
saas.description: Fully managed — no infrastructure to operate, fastest to start.

self_hosted.label: Self-hosted
self_hosted.description: You run it — full data control, ideal for strict compliance or cost at scale.

flexible.label: Either works
flexible.description: Open to both — we'll factor in other dimensions more.

step_features.title: Which capabilities matter most?
step_features.subtitle: Select all that apply. We'll score vendors on how well they support these.

enterprise_sso.label: Enterprise SSO
enterprise_sso.description: SAML 2.0, per-customer SSO connections for B2B.

social_login.label: Social Login
social_login.description: Sign in with Google, Apple, GitHub, and more.

passwordless.label: Passwordless / Magic Links
passwordless.description: One-time codes and magic links via email or SMS.

adaptive_mfa.label: Adaptive / Risk-Based MFA
adaptive_mfa.description: MFA triggered by anomalous signals (device, location, IP).

m2m.label: Machine-to-Machine (M2M)
m2m.description: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials for service-to-service auth.

custom_branding.label: Custom Branding
custom_branding.description: Fully customisable login pages and email templates.

step_compliance.title: Any regulatory requirements?
step_compliance.subtitle: Select all that apply. Vendors without the required certifications will be ranked lower.

soc2.label: SOC 2 Type II
soc2.description: Security and availability audits for SaaS companies.

hipaa.label: HIPAA
hipaa.description: US healthcare data protection — requires a BAA.

pci.label: PCI DSS
pci.description: Payment card industry data security standard.

step_budget.title: What's your monthly budget?
step_budget.subtitle: This helps filter out vendors that may be out of range for your stage.

enterprise.label: Enterprise budget
enterprise.description: Custom pricing negotiable — scale and SLAs matter more than cost.

openidProviders.title: OpenID Connect Providers Benchmark
openidProviders.description: Compare OpenID Connect providers across grant types, token endpoint authentication methods, extensions, endpoints, FAPI compliance, and protocol features.
